measures.name: ORDEREDPALLETSMEASURE
measures.description: The ORDEREDPALLETSMEASURE measure calculates the total number of ordered pallets by summing the values in the ORDEREDPALLETS column from the OPENORDERS table. This metric is used to understand the total volume of pallets ordered across all open orders.

measures.name: M-DIF
measures.description: The M-DIF measure calculates the ratio of quantities packed to quantities ordered, specifically for packing slips. This measure is used to understand the packing efficiency, ensuring that items are being packed in accordance with the quantities ordered.

measures.name: C-DIF
measures.description:
Variables:
CUSTOMERCOLES: Checks if the current invoice account is "CUS0027".
CUSTOMERCOLESHB: Checks if the current invoice account is "CUS0001".
CUSTOMERWW: Checks if the current invoice account is "CUS0044".
SWITCH Function: Evaluates multiple conditions and returns the corresponding result.
If CUSTOMERWW is true, it returns the average of GPOA_% from the WWGPOA table.
If CUSTOMERCOLES or CUSTOMERCOLESHB is true, it returns the average of DIF from the CUSTOMERCASEFILL table, but capped at a maximum value of 1.
If none of the conditions are met, it returns BLANK().

measures.name: C-DIFOT
measures.description:
Variables:
CUSTOMERCOLES: Checks if the current invoice account is "CUS0027".
CUSTOMERCOLESHB: Checks if the current invoice account is "CUS0001".
CUSTOMERWW: Checks if the current invoice account is "CUS0044".
SWITCH Function: Evaluates multiple conditions and returns the corresponding result.
For CUSTOMERWW, it calculates the percentage of received on time cases by dividing the sum of RECEIVEDONTIME by the sum of ORDERED.
For CUSTOMERCOLES and CUSTOMERCOLESHB, it also calculates the percentage of received on time cases, but caps the result at 1 if the received on time count exceeds the ordered count.
If none of the conditions are met, it returns BLANK().

measures.name: M-DIFOT
measures.description: The M-DIFOT measure calculates a combined metric that multiplies the M-DIF measure (the ratio of packed to ordered quantities) with the M-DOT measure (another performance metric), but only if there are packed orders. If there are no packed orders, it returns a blank value.

measures.name: M-PACKSLIPORDERED
measures.description: The M-PACKSLIPORDERED measure calculates the total quantity of items ORDERED according to the packing slips. It sums the values in the QTY column from the PACKINGSLIPS table.

measures.name: M-PACKSLIPDELIVERED
measures.description: The M-PACKSLIPDELIVERED measure calculates the total quantity of items delivered according to the packing slips. It sums the values in the QTY column from the PACKINGSLIPS table.

measures.name: M-DOT
measures.description: The M-DOT measure calculates the average on-time performance of packing slips, but only if there are packed orders. If there are no packed orders, it returns a blank value.

measures.name: REASON
measures.description: The REASON measure retrieves the lexicographically greatest (i.e., alphabetically last) value from the CATEGORYID column in the CASES table.

measures.name: PALLETBREAKFLAG
measures.description:
SWITCH Function: This DAX function evaluates multiple conditions and returns the corresponding result.
The first argument, TRUE(), ensures that the conditions are evaluated as logical tests.
Conditions and Results:
If MAX(OPENORDERS[PALLETBREAK]) equals "ONE", the measure returns 1.
If MAX(OPENORDERS[PALLETBREAK]) equals "2TO11", the measure returns 2.
If MAX(OPENORDERS[PALLETBREAK]) equals "22TO44", the measure returns 3.
If MAX(OPENORDERS[PALLETBREAK]) equals "FTL", the measure returns 4.
If none of the conditions are met, the measure returns BLANK().

measures.name: TARGETS
measures.description:
For C-DIF and M-DIF when the respective ordered quantities are greater than zero, the target is set to 0.95.
For C-DIFOT and M-DIFOT when the respective ordered quantities are greater than zero, the target is set to 0.90.
For C-DOT and M-DOT when the respective ordered quantities are greater than zero, the target is set to 0.90.
For DC-SOH, the target is BLANK().
For DC-DAYSCOVER when ordered quantities are greater than zero, the target is set to 10.
If none of the conditions are met, the measure returns BLANK().

measures.name: SALESORDEREDPALLETS
measures.description: The SALESORDEREDPALLETS measure calculates the total number of pallets ordered by summing the values in the ORDEREDPALLETS column from the SALESORDERS table. This metric is used to understand the total pallet volume associated with sales orders.

measures.name: C-DCSOH
measures.description: The DCSOH measure calculates the total stock on hand (SOH) by summing the values in the SOH column of the DCDAYSCOVER table. This metric is typically used to understand the total amount of inventory currently available.

measures.name: C-DCDAYSCOVER
measures.description: The DCDAYSCOVER measure calculates the average days cover value from the DCDAYSCOVER table. This metric is typically used to understand how many days of inventory are available based on current stock levels and usage rates.

measures.name: COUNTOFORDERS
measures.description: The COUNTOFORDERS measure calculates the total number of distinct sales orders that are currently open.

measures.name: SALESORDERASOFDATE
measures.description: The SALESORDERASOFDATE measure calculates the most recent date and time a sales order was processed and adds 10 hours to that timestamp.

measures.name: PACKSLIPASOFDATE
measures.description: The PACKSLIPASOFDATE measure calculates the most recent date and time a packing slip was processed, then adds 10 hours to that timestamp.

measures.name: C-ORDERED
measures.description:
Variables:
CUSTOMERCOLES: Checks if the current invoice account is "CUS0027".
CUSTOMERCOLESHB: Checks if the current invoice account is "CUS0001".
CUSTOMERWW: Checks if the current invoice account is "CUS0044".
SWITCH Function: Evaluates multiple conditions and returns the corresponding result.
For CUSTOMERWW, it sums the ORDERED values from the WWGPOA table.
For CUSTOMERCOLES and CUSTOMERCOLESHB, it sums the ORDERED values from the CUSTOMERCASEFILL table.
If none of the conditions are met, it returns BLANK().

measures.name: C-RECEIVED
measures.description:
Variables:
CUSTOMERCOLES: Checks if the current invoice account is "CUS0027".
CUSTOMERCOLESHB: Checks if the current invoice account is "CUS0001".
CUSTOMERWW: Checks if the current invoice account is "CUS0044".
SWITCH Function: Evaluates multiple conditions and returns the corresponding result.
For CUSTOMERWW, it sums the RECEIVED values from the WWGPOA table.
For CUSTOMERCOLES and CUSTOMERCOLESHB, it sums the RECEIVED values from the CUSTOMERCASEFILL table.
If none of the conditions are met, it returns BLANK().

measures.name: C-RECEIVEDONTIME
measures.description:
Variables:
CUSTOMERCOLES: Checks if the current invoice account is "CUS0027".
CUSTOMERCOLESHB: Checks if the current invoice account is "CUS0001".
CUSTOMERWW: Checks if the current invoice account is "CUS0044".
SWITCH Function: Evaluates multiple conditions and returns the corresponding result.
For CUSTOMERWW, it sums the RECEIVEDONTIME values from the CUSTOMERCASEFILL table.
For CUSTOMERCOLES, it sums the RECEIVEDONTIME values from the CUSTOMERCASEFILL table.
For CUSTOMERCOLESHB, it sums the RECEIVEDONTIME values from the CUSTOMERCASEFILL table.
If none of the conditions are met, it returns BLANK().

measures.name: COUNTOFORDERS PY
measures.description:
The `COUNTOFORDERS PY` measure calculates the total number of orders for the previous fiscal year. Here's how it works:

1. **CALCULATE([COUNTOFORDERS], ...)**: This part calculates the total number of orders using the `[COUNTOFORDERS]` measure.

2. **'.DATES'[FISCALYEAR] = SELECTEDVALUE('.DATES'[FISCALYEAR], BLANK()) - 1)**: This filter selects the previous fiscal year by taking the current fiscal year from the `'.DATES'[FISCALYEAR]` column, subtracting 1, and applying it as a filter.

The result is the count of orders for the previous fiscal year, allowing for year-over-year comparisons.

measures.name: FTLEFFICIENCY
measures.description:
The `FTLEFFICIENCY` measure calculates the efficiency of orders by comparing the count of orders under the current filters to the total count of all orders, regardless of specific filters like `PALLETBREAK` or `DCNAME`. Here's how it works:

1. **DIVIDE([COUNTOFORDERS], ... , BLANK())**: Divides the count of orders under the current filters (`[COUNTOFORDERS]`) by the total count of all orders. If the denominator is zero, it returns `BLANK()`.

2. **CALCULATE([COUNTOFORDERS], ALL(OPENORDERS[PALLETBREAK]), ALL('.DCADDRESS'[DCNAME]))**: Removes any filters on `OPENORDERS[PALLETBREAK]` and `.DCADDRESS[DCNAME]` to get the total count of all orders.

The result is a ratio that represents the efficiency of orders under the current context, comparing it to the total possible orders. This measure can be used to assess how effectively orders are being managed relative to the full capacity or potential.

measures.name: CountOfAllOrders
measures.description:
The `CountOfAllOrders` measure calculates the total number of orders by using the `[COUNTOFORDERS]` measure. It removes any filters on the `OPENORDERS[PALLETBREAK]` and `.DCADDRESS[DCNAME]` columns to ensure the count includes all orders, regardless of pallet breaks or distribution center names.

This measure provides a total count of orders across the entire dataset, ignoring specific filters that might otherwise limit the count.

measures.name: FTLEFFICIENCY PY
measures.description:
The `FTLEFFICIENCY PY` measure calculates the full truckload efficiency (`[FTLEFFICIENCY]`) for the previous fiscal year. Here's how it works:

1. **CALCULATE([FTLEFFICIENCY], ...)**: Computes the `FTLEFFICIENCY` measure but applies the following filter.

2. **'.DATES'[FISCALYEAR] = SELECTEDVALUE('.DATES'[FISCALYEAR], BLANK()) - 1)**: Filters the data to include only the previous fiscal year by taking the current fiscal year from `'.DATES'[FISCALYEAR]`, subtracting 1, and applying it as a filter.

The result is the full truckload efficiency for the previous fiscal year, allowing for comparisons with the current fiscal year's efficiency.

measures.name: FULLFILMENTEFFICIENCY
measures.description: The `FULLFILMENTEFFICIENCY` measure calculates the ratio of unique sales orders in the current context to the total unique sales orders across all warehouses. It measures how efficiently orders are being fulfilled in the specific context compared to the total potential across all warehouses.
